title: Fix Claude CLI false timeout detection in Keiro pipeline
description:
ClaudeCli.run reports 'claude timed out after 300000ms' even when Claude actually completes work (creates branches, pushes, opens PRs). The pipeline treats this as a failure, triggering TQM and circuit breaker unnecessarily.

Root cause: ClaudeCli.run's result detection does not properly capture Claude's output/exit status. Successful runs are misreported as timeouts.

Evidence:
- Run 1: lowendinsight-1mn (health endpoint) — reported timeout, but Claude created branch feat/v1-health-endpoint and PR #41
- Run 2: lowendinsight-wka (rate limiting) — reported timeout, Claude may have done work on feat/scope-ratelimit-registration
- Run 2: lowendinsight-52 (TQM circuit breaker) — reported timeout, but Claude created PR #42

Fix location: lib/keiro/eng/claude_cli.ex — investigate how ClaudeCli.run captures the exit code and stdout from the claude process. The 300s timeout in System.cmd or Port may be firing before Claude finishes, or the result parsing may not handle Claude's output format correctly.

title: Add admin dashboard for cache visibility and usage monitoring
description:
Add a simple admin view at /admin (protected by admin auth) that shows:

1. **Cache overview**
   - Total cached entries (count from ETS via LowendinsightGet.Datastore)
   - Cache hit/miss ratio (from UsageTracker or new counters)
   - Top cached repos (most frequently accessed)
   - Entries nearing TTL expiry

2. **Usage monitoring**
   - Recent API requests (last 100, with timestamp, endpoint, org/key, repo URL)
   - Per-org usage summary (total requests, cache hits, cache misses)
   - Request rate (requests/minute over last hour)

3. **Active sessions**
   - In-progress analysis jobs (from AnalysisSupervisor)
   - Job queue depth

Implementation approach:
- Add /admin route to endpoint.ex, protected by a simple admin token (LEI_ADMIN_TOKEN env var)
- Create admin.html.eex template using existing Bulma CSS
- Add a simple ETS-based request logger (ring buffer of last 1000 requests)
- Read cache stats from existing Datastore.export_cache() stats
- Read usage from existing UsageTracker.get_current_usage()
- No database changes needed — read from existing ETS tables and UsageTracker

Files:
- apps/lowendinsight_get/lib/lowendinsight_get/endpoint.ex (add /admin route)
- apps/lowendinsight_get/priv/templates/admin.html.eex (new template)
- apps/lowendinsight_get/lib/lowendinsight_get/request_logger.ex (new, optional)
- apps/lowendinsight_get/lib/lowendinsight_get/datastore.ex (add cache_stats/0 if needed)

Acceptance: /admin with valid token shows cache contents, usage stats, and active jobs. Unauthenticated requests get 401.
notes: IMPORTANT: When implementing, generate the admin token and include it in the PR description and bead comment so the operator can retrieve it. The deploy bead should set it via `fly secrets set LEI_ADMIN_TOKEN=<value> -a lowendinsight`. The token value MUST be surfaced in the bead comments — do not generate a secret without recording it.

title: Restore GitHub Trending Analysis feature
description:
The GitHub Trending Analysis feature at /gh_trending/:language is broken. Selecting a language (e.g. /gh_trending/elixir) returns a JSON error instead of the trending analysis report.

The route exists in apps/lowendinsight_get/lib/lowendinsight_get/endpoint.ex (line 80) and calls LowendinsightGet.GithubTrending.get_current_gh_trending_report(language), but the response is being caught by a different handler, returning the generic POST error message.

Investigation needed:
1. Check if the GithubTrending module still exists and works after the umbrella migration (PR #33)
2. Check route ordering in endpoint.ex — the gh_trending GET route may be shadowed by a catch-all
3. Verify the trending data source (GitHub scraping) is still functional
4. Check if the language.html.eex template is being rendered correctly

The /gh_trending index page loads fine and shows the language dropdown. The issue is specifically with /gh_trending/:language returning results.

Files:
- apps/lowendinsight_get/lib/lowendinsight_get/endpoint.ex (routes)
- apps/lowendinsight_get/lib/lowendinsight_get/github_trending.ex (data source)
- apps/lowendinsight_get/priv/templates/language.html.eex (results template)

Acceptance: Clicking a language on /gh_trending shows the trending repos analysis. No regressions to other endpoints.
